_note: Timecodes are APPROXIMATE — source transcript had no per-line timing. Run Whisper on the pulled audio (or use YouTube's timestamped transcript) and search each clip's locatorQuote to set exact inSec/outSec and regenerate word-level captions. Each clip flagged needsTimecode:true.

clips.locatorQuote: PBMs are allowed to have contracts where you're not paying that pass-through amount

captions.text: PBMs are allowed to have contracts where you're not paying that pass-through amount. They could pay the pharmacy one price for that GLP-1, then invoice you a higher amount and keep the difference. That's spread pricing. And not just for GLP-1s, for everything.

clips.locatorQuote: after three years, it's like one in 12 people are still using a GLP-1

captions.text: After three years, it's like one in 12 people are still using a GLP-1. That's not necessarily bad, if they had the weight loss and lifestyle and came off but sustained it. But if it's because they can't afford it or can't tolerate it, that's a very different situation.

clips.locatorQuote: a lot of PBMs will tell you that if you add in lifestyle requirements

captions.text: A lot of PBMs will tell you that if you add lifestyle requirements, they can't or won't do it, because it impacts their rebates and they claw those rebates back. It's very sad they won't model what actually worked, and invest in the member's long-term health.

clips.locatorQuote: about a third of employees would consider moving jobs if they could get GLP-1 coverage

captions.text: About a third of employees would consider moving jobs if they could get GLP-1 coverage. That's a huge population. It's 30%. So if I'm a leader or an owner, and I hear that, I want to keep my good people.

clips.locatorQuote: members are going to find a way to get those drugs regardless

captions.text: Members are going to find a way to get those drugs regardless of whether you do it. So whether you cover it or not, all of this is happening. There's no way to just put your head in the sand and think this is going away.
